witnesses.description: Exact natural cubic spline replay for knots 0,1,2 with values 0,1,0, evaluated on the left interval at x=1/2.
Exact natural cubic spline replay on the right interval at x=3/2.
Exact replay of value, first-derivative, second-derivative, and natural-boundary constraints for the same spline.

checks.claim: The listed natural cubic spline evaluates to 11/16 at x=1/2 on the left interval.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes piece endpoint values, derivative values, interior smoothness, natural endpoint constraints, and the exact spline value.

checks.claim: The listed natural cubic spline evaluates to 11/16 at x=3/2 on the right interval.
checks.notes: The symmetric right-interval row checks that the second piece satisfies the same exact assembly constraints.

checks.claim: The listed natural cubic spline is value-, first-derivative-, and second-derivative-continuous at the interior knot, with natural endpoint second derivatives.
checks.notes: This row checks the finite spline assembly constraints without claiming general spline existence or uniqueness.

checks.claim: For the left-midpoint natural spline row, the spline value is 3/4.
checks.notes: Exact replay computes the spline value as 11/16, so the claimed value 3/4 is rejected; the separate qf-lra-bad-spline-value row owns the checked Farkas evidence.

checks.claim: The fixed malformed spline-value claim is linearly inconsistent.

data.certificate: The resource regression parses the source SMT-LIB artifact, emits UnsatFarkas evidence, and independently checks the certificate.

checks.notes: Exact replay computes the spline value as 11/16, while the malformed row claims 3/4; the source QF_LRA artifact isolates that scalar contradiction for Farkas checking.

checks.claim: General cubic spline existence, uniqueness, boundary-condition theory, error estimates, convergence, shape preservation, and floating-point spline evaluation require future theorem or numerical-honesty resources.

data.target_theorem_shape: For cubic spline interpolation, prove existence and uniqueness for valid knot data and boundary conditions, smoothness, error formulas, convergence, shape-preservation hypotheses, and approximation claims separately from fixed rational replay rows.
data.future_checker: Lean theorem statements for spline spaces and interpolation theory plus separate numerical-honesty or QF_FP artifacts for floating-point spline implementations.

checks.notes: The finite exact rows are checked resources for one rational natural-spline assembly, not theorem evidence for arbitrary knots, boundary conditions, spline spaces, error bounds, convergence, or floating-point implementations.
